_article: XXXIX.7 — Settlement Graph is a first-class compiled artifact. User directive: 'Wang Village located_in Zhao, connected_to Heng Yue, fed_by Spirit Vein A, protected_by Zhao Patrol, supplied_by Farmland 3, threatened_by Wolves.'
_description: Schema for the COMPILED Settlement Graph — the civilizational viability graph where nodes are Settlements (villages/towns/cities/sects/strongholds) and edges are supply/protection/threat/dependency relationships. This is the graph the simulation traverses to answer 'is Wang Village thriving or dying?' by checking its suppliers, defenders, threats, and spirit vein — not by reading a static 'health' field.

nodes.settlement_class: enum — village | town | city | sect | stronghold | outpost | market | caravanserai | harbor | shrine

viability_state.population_class: enum — handful | small | medium | large | sprawling
viability_state.decline_or_ascent_trend: enum — ascending | stable | stagnating | declining | collapsing | abandoned
viability_state.defense_class: enum — undefended | militia | walled | garrisoned | sect_protected
viability_state.cultivation_viability: float 0.0–1.0 — driven by spirit_graph (can cultivators progress here?)

edges.type: enum — supplied_by | protected_by | threatened_by | fed_by (spirit vein) | governed_by | allied_with | trades_with | recruits_from | shelters (refugee edge when a settlement falls)
edges.strength: float 0.0–1.0 — how dependent / how strong the protection / how severe the threat
edges.live: bool — is this edge currently active? (a defeated threat edge becomes live=false; a destroyed bridge makes a supplied_by edge live=false)

indices.by_threat: map<threat_id, [settlement_id]> — what is this threat menacing
indices.by_supplier: map<supplier_id, [settlement_id]> — what does this supplier support

queries_supported: viability(settlement_id) -> {supply_edges, protection_edges, threat_edges, vein_edge} — full profile
is_thriving(settlement_id) -> bool — derived: supply stable, protection >= threat, vein healthy
is_dying(settlement_id) -> bool — derived: supply disrupted OR protection < threat OR vein depleting
cascade_if_lost(settlement_id, edge_type, edge_id) -> [consequence] — what happens to this settlement if this edge is severed
decline_drivers(settlement_id) -> [edge_id] — which edges are driving decline (for NPC reasoning: 'why is the village dying?')

_compile_source: World Blueprint DSL v2 — Settlement entities + supplied_by/protected_by/threatened_by/fed_by/governed_by/allied_with/trades_with/recruits_from relationships
